key_insight: A constrained LLM front end can safely translate natural language into finite-element simulation inputs while keeping solver code deterministic and human-written.
engineering_relevance: This design lowers setup effort for multi-physics solid mechanics simulations without giving the model control over the numerical core, improving reliability for engineering workflows.
method: LLM only parses prompts into structured JSON and generates Gmsh code for non-catalog geometries.
A deterministic dispatcher routes the validated specification to five fixed FEniCS/UFL templates.
Retry/validation logic repairs parser and geometry errors before simulation execution.
evidence_signal: On a 15-prompt parsing benchmark, final valid parse rate reached 100% with 100% problem-class accuracy and 97.1% field-extraction accuracy; custom-geometry tests achieved 90% final success.
watch_item: The approach still depends on prompt validation and can fail on invalid custom geometries, so it is not fully open-ended automation.
paper_title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
